Stuart and Rainie go out for dinner, but he becomes increasingly concerned about her behaviour. Adam invites Habiba out, only for Honey to suggest they spend some time together(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals - a tough time for some, but for others a food bonanza. Jasari and his wild dog family are under siege from the hyena clan, so he must train his pups to survive, while Bakari the baboon is living in exile from the troop, only to spy the leader bullying his adopted baby. Kali and her cubs are enjoying life back in the heart of the pride, only to be attacked by the black-maned lions. But worse is to come when a storm ignites a devastating fire, consuming the dry land with flames(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: The late-in-life comedy starring Alison Steadman and John Cleese returns for its second run. With their retirement together waiting on the sale of his house, Phil despairs of finding the right estate agent. The current one suggests an 'open day', but Phil baulks at the idea. Edith, meanwhile, is heartened to find that her son Roger is earning some sort of a living, even if it's only busking. Queenie confesses to Edith that she can't bear the thought of her going away, and when she hears that plans are currently stalled by the difficulty of selling Phil's house, she sees her chance to influence events(n)

With excuses thin on the ground, the gang reluctantly join the duo on their outdoor adventure. Will they regret the decision?(n)

programme.title: The Secret History of the British Garden
programme.desc: Monty Don examines how British gardens have changed over the past 400 years. The first episode focuses on the 17th century, and features a visit to Levens Hall in Cumbria, the only garden from the 1600s that survives to the present day. He also examines archaeological evidence of a lost garden and uncovers hidden message and religious meaning in an aerial photograph taken during the Second World War. Finally, he pays a visit to Hampton Court to see the influence of the Civil War on garden design(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish
programme.desc: Documentary following life in the British overseas territory in the south Atlantic, where it is autumn and the Rev Richard Hines is preparing for Palm Sunday, which involves collecting enough tussock grass to make 100 crosses by hand. Over on Saunders Island, the Pole-Evans family are preparing to send the sheep off to market, while the islanders are ready to celebrate the Queen's birthday with a parade and reception at Government House(n)

programme.title: Britain in Bloom
programme.desc: Chris Bavin meets the gardeners of Evington in Leicester hoping to win their first gold, including elderly volunteers who have revived an overgrown area of the local park and members of a gardening club who have transformed a strip of land at a primary school into a bee-friendly wildlife area(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo picks up his rail journey from Warwick to Rye. In Reading, he traces the origins of education for workers, and in Cliveden explores a great Italianate mansion(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers explain how necessity often forced cooks to make the most of limited ingredients to create tasty dishes. They reveal that many popular meals were invented during hard times, from mince and dumplings to Scotch broths(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: A critically ill miniature donkey foal makes an emotional emergency case for equine vet Kerry, and Richard creates an operating table from a forklift truck. At the Small Animal Hospital, senior vet Adam's job is anything but routine when he X-rays a tortoise and treats a gecko with a problem eye. Narrated by Felicity Kendal(n)

programme.title: Gorilla Family and Me
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gordon Buchanan returns to the Democratic Republic of Congo in the hope of finding the gorilla family again, and discovering the outcome of silverback Chimanuka's confrontation with a rival. He soon finds the apes, but discovers their lives are now under threat from poachers, and works with the local rangers to prevent them coming to harm(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw visit Birmingham, where one grisly find features a gallows and a break from the shops uncovers the story of the real Peaky Blinders(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Singers and performers Bobby Crush, Nicholas McCarthy, Sonia, Sandi Bogle and Sam Bailey take on the general-knowledge champions. Hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Jay Blades and the team restore more family heirlooms, including a tired and dilapidated a rocking horse that holds huge sentimental significance for its owner and a 1930s pinball machine. Other items are a Victorian device used for measuring weather pressure and a pair of precious plates(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary series following the ups and downs of six farming families in Scotland, from Argyll to the Outer Hebrides, as they face up to tough economic times and an uncertain future. Crofting couple Lynn and Sandra face the danger of a drought, while buffalo farmer Stevie's herd go for a cooling dip in the loch. At the Royal Highland Show, Raymond and Johnny are hoping for success with their Charolais cattle, while Aimee puts her pony skills to the test(n)

programme.title: Broke
programme.desc: Documentary following more people who are in work but struggling to make ends meet. They include Tyrone, a homeless teenager who works a zero-hour contract at McDonald's and wants to rent his own flat - but cannot guarantee he will earn enough each week to cover the rent. Plus, the story of Ecuadorian immigrant Angelica, who lives in London, where she studies English all day and works night shifts as a cleaner. After requesting a pay rise to the living wage, she was suspended by her employment agency(n)

programme.title: Vic & Bob's Big Night Out
programme.desc: Bob Mortimer showcases his incredible new dance routine and eventually forces a reluctant Vic Reeves to join in. Plus, characters Donald and Davey Stott take to the stage to perform an ambitious magic routine, and The Man With The Stick makes a long awaited return, before Vic and Bob end the show with a performance of their song, You Can Do It(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events. Presented by Emma Barnett(n)

programme.title: Golf: Women's British Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from day one at Woburn Golf Club, near Milton Keynes, where England's Georgia Hall entered as the defending champion(n)

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Surviving America's Most Hated Family
programme.desc: Thirteen years since first encountering one of America's most notorious hate groups, award-winning film-maker Louis Theroux makes a long-anticipated return to Kansas to spend time with the Westboro Baptist Church - a hugely-controversial Christian ministry that for years has picketed at military funerals and other high-profile events with deliberately provocative and homophobic placards. Immersing himself in the strange world of Westboro, Louis explores what happens when a hate-group largely populated by one family loses its patriarch(n)

programme.title: Our Cops in the North
programme.desc: Observational documentary following the work of Northumbria Police across Newcastle, Sunderland and beyond, telling stories of the force's officers and victims of crime. In the first episode, police in Sunderland come to the aid of a 70-year-old who has been robbed at knife-point by masked assailants, who have made off with £15,000 in cash. In a secluded hamlet where sheep outnumber people, PC Curtis Ritchie investigates the theft of a vintage Land Rover Defender, which turns out to be one of many vehicles stolen on the same night(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Secret History of the British Garden
programme.desc: Monty Don turns his attention to the 18th century, and pays a visit to Croome Castle in Worcestershire, the first commission of the celebrated garden designer Lancelot 'Capability' Brown. The era saw a revolution in gardening and the creation of the first landscape gardens. Monty examines the factors that influenced and inspired these new developments, including a recipe for clay, and learns how to mow with a scythe. He also meets the present Duke of Devonshire, who is restoring his land to its 18th-century design(n)

programme.title: An Island Parish
programme.desc: As Easter week arrives, the Reverend Richard lends a hand in Stanley's only bakery preparing the hot cross buns. Over on West Falkland, farmer Critta Lee indulges in some last-minute practice with his sheep dogs Duke and Bitzer prior to the inter-island sheep dog trials. Meanwhile, taxidermist and animal artist Steve Massam puts the finishing touches to his first attempt of a human sculpture - a bronze bust of Margaret Thatcher(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty Don catches up with summer maintenance in his wildlife garden and recommends plants for dry shade under trees. Carol Klein travels to Aberglasney Gardens in south-west Wales to explore the best planting partners for that most ephemeral of perennials, the Thalictrum, while Joe Swift is in Devon meeting a plantsman who has used plants from all over the globe to create an extraordinary garden. Plus, Adam Frost joins a group of gardeners in Worksop where gardening has helped to create a sense of community and wellbeing, and Nick Bailey finds out more about the lily beetle(n)

programme.title: Getting the Builders In
programme.desc: In Epsom, Surrey, homeowners Zoe and Kevin want to get the builders in to transform an empty shell into a stylish en suite bathroom. The builders offer up three very different designs. Elliott and Paul have a clever tiling idea to make the room feel bigger, John and Simon come up with a sleek, minimalist design, and Martin and Mick think their plan for underfloor heating should clinch them the deal. Zoe and Kevin have £7,500 to spend - which builders will they pick? In Didsbury, Greater Manchester, Jane and John need bespoke wardrobes for the bedroom(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerrige, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare a selection of extravagant British gourmet dishes, using high-quality ingredients including lobster, truffles, prime cuts of meat and expensive chocolate. The duo also explore the stories behind the recipes(n)

programme.title: More Creatures Great and Small
programme.desc: Small-animal vet Adam decides his Shetland pony Hector should have an operation to make him less aggressive, and Steve helps a border collie with a womb infection. In Teesdale, farm vet Graham discovers a cow is having difficulty giving birth to twins(n)

programme.title: Natural World
programme.sub-title: Meet the Moose Family: Natural World
programme.desc: The nature documentary tells the story of a mother moose bringing up her calf in the wilds of the Canadian rockies. Many challenges lie ahead for the mother and son, not least from the threat of the hungry bears and wolves that live nearby. Film-maker Hugo Kitching sets out to follow the duo through the four seasons, although things take an unexpected turn later in the year with the discovery of a third moose(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and Paul Laidlaw peruse antique shops in Oxfordshire, Gloucestershire, Warwickshire and Northamptonshire as they head to the final auction in Middle Littleton, Worcestershire. Plus, a visit to Shakespeare's birthplace in Stratford-upon-Avon to find out about a 19th-century, Bard-inspired, American black actor(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Eggheads
programme.desc: Authors Wendy Holden, Dr Kate Williams, Jenny Colgan, Noreena Hertz and Dreda Say Mitchell hope to win money for their charity in the quiz hosted by Jeremy Vine(n)

programme.title: The Customer Is Always Right
programme.desc: British entrepreneurs' products are put to the test, with consumers unboxing, trying out and giving their feedback on each invention(n)

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: The writer and adventurer continues his tour around Wales by moving to both mid-Wales and the west of the country. From a lead mine that's thousands of years old and a beautiful cave with a very intimidating entrance to an abandoned sea fort and a derelict mental asylum, Will uncovers historical gems that reveal parts of the middle and west of Wales that most people don't know exists(n)

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Comedy actors Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer travel to the picturesque River Usk in south Wales, where Paul's father first taught him to fish as a young child. The duo discuss their respective childhoods and reflect on their own family lives and values. In their cosy cottage accommodation in the heart of the countryside they compare school photographs and Paul serenades Bob with his guitar. Once outside in the picturesque environment, their quarry is the wild brown trout(n)

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: At Longmeadow, Monty Don catches up on jobs to keep the garden looking good all summer long. Adam Frost visits Sledmere Gardens in East Yorkshire where the borders have been planted with specific colour schemes in mind. The programme also catches up with a family in the Vale of Glamorgan whose members pack their small garden with colourful bedding plants for maximum impact(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: Drama, based on true-life facts, recounting the romantic and creative partnership between influential choreographer-director Bob Fosse, and Gwen Verdon, one of the greatest Broadway dancers of all time. The story begins in 1966, mid-way through the couple's marriage, with the launch of the film Sweet Charity, directed by Fosse and based on the successful Broadway musical that had originally starred Verdon. Dining with producer Cy Feuer, Bob puts himself forward as the director of Cy's next project - Cabaret. Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams star(n)

programme.title: Fosse/Verdon
programme.desc: While staying in Majorca, Gwen considers her relationship with Bob, whom she has caught being unfaithful during the filming of Cabaret. She reflects on meeting Fosse years earlier when she had been offered the lead in the musical Damn Yankees when Fosse had insisted on auditioning her. The pair immediately hit it off, placing pressure on Fosse's marriage to the actress Joan McCracken, who was unwell at the time. Bob tells Gwen that he wants to come home, but he is still in love with the German translator Hannah. True-life drama, starring Sam Rockwell and Michelle Williams(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: The day's national and international news stories, with Mark Urban(n)

programme.title: Weather
programme.desc: No Details Available(n)

programme.title: Golf: Women's British Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from day two at Woburn Golf Club, near Milton Keynes, where England's Georgia Hall entered as the defending champion(n)

programme.title: A Fresh Guide to Florence with Fab 5 Freddy
programme.desc: Hip-hop pioneer Fred Brathwaite, better known to music fans as Fab 5 Freddy, who was part of a creative revolution during the 1970s and 1980s, goes back in time to the 15th and 16th centuries to examine the multi-racial society that played a part in the Italian Renaissance but have seemingly fallen through the cracks of history. With so many Renaissance artists representing black figures of prominence in their work, Freddy aims to bring focus back to the social change at the heart of the art movement and artists who were the cultural disrupters of their time(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: The Boeing 737 Max was set to dominate the skies, but two deadly crashes have left the aircraft grounded and the company in crisis. Both flights were forced down by software specially designed for the new aircraft. Reporter Richard Bilton investigates the plane's fatal flaws and asks whether Boeing should have done more to protect passengers(n)

programme.title: A Cut Too Far? Male Circumcision
programme.desc: An investigation into the rights and wrongs of male circumcision, one of the most common surgical procedures in the world. In the UK, thousands of baby boys are circumcised every year for religious or cultural reasons. But it is becoming increasingly controversial. Journalist Adnan Sarwar, who was circumcised by his Muslim parents as an infant, presents this investigation into the operation and its safety, finding out whether it is an outmoded practice or a harmless expression of cultural and religious freedoms(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: Including a visit to the World Thumb Wrestling Championships in Norwich(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The presenters look back at some of our amazing dens from previous series(n)

programme.title: Mustangs FC
programme.desc: After encouraging Nish's disastrous tactics, Marnie rallies the troops as they face a hostile team(n)

programme.title: Deadly Top 10
programme.desc: Steve Backshall counts down the 10 creatures with the most developed senses, including killer taste buds and a crocodile with a motion-detecting jaw(n)

programme.title: Monkey Planet
programme.desc: Zoologist George McGavin concludes his exploration of the primate world, considering their capacity to use tools and solve problems, something he claims sets them apart from most other families on the planet. By way of demonstration, the presenter goes to orang-utan school in Sumatra, visits Thailand to see long-tailed macaques floss their teeth with human hair and use tools to open oysters on the beach, and reveals how Ugandan chimpanzees pass on cultures and customs down the generations. He also meets a resourceful bonobo in the States that can order its own picnic on the phone and build a fire, on which it then toasts marshmallows(n)

programme.title: Cats v Dogs: Which Is Best?
programme.desc: Chris Packham and Liz Bonnin present the first of two programmes in which they put their canine chums and feline friends under the microscope, with the aim of answering the age-old question - which are best, cats or dogs? The animals go head to head in a series of rounds testing different aspects, beginning in this edition with intelligence, sensory powers and physical prowess. On the search for answers, dog-lover Chris comes face to face with a pack of wolves, while feline fan Liz confronts an Arabian wild cat to discover how the relationship between humans and their pets have evolved(n)

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Documentary series following the ups and downs of six farming families in Scotland and Northern Ireland as they face up to tough economic times and an uncertain future. Crofting couple Lynn Cassells and Sandra Baer face the danger of a drought, while buffalo farmer Stevie Mitchell's herd go for a cooling dip in the loch. At the Royal Highland Show, hill farmer Johnny Irvine and his son Raymond are hoping for success with their Charolais cattle, while Aimee Budge from Shetland puts her pony skills to the test(n)

programme.title: Secret Britain
programme.desc: Ellie Harrison and Adam Henson go hunting for hidden corners of the UK landscape and meet some of the people who live there. They begin by exploring the unseen water world of the Brecon Beacons in south Wales, where Ellie confronts her fear of tight spaces to venture into Britain's deepest cave - carved through the rock by an ancient underground river - and a couple reveal how their lives were transformed when they bought a 200-year-old toll bridge over the River Wye. There are also visits to a mysterious pond known as the Witches' Pool and a magical walkway behind a waterfall(n)

programme.title: The Red Shoes
programme.desc: A budding prima ballerina is set to achieve her dream of stardom when she decides to leave her prestigious company to marry a composer - a decision that enrages her possessive mentor, who vows revenge. Powell and Pressburger's drama, based on the story by Hans Christian Andersen, starring Anton Walbrook, Moira Shearer, Marius Goring, Robert Helpmann and Albert Basserman(n)

credits.actor: Anton Walbrook, Moira Shearer, Marius Goring, Robert Helpmann, Albert Basserman

programme.title: Galapagos
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin joins a research expedition across the Galapagos Islands, the vast archipelago where evolution has proceeded at an extraordinary pace. The journey begins on the west side, home to a richly diverse wildlife scene, where Liz and the scientists journey into the clouds above the islands' tallest volcano on a hunt for the endangered pink iguana. Liz also travels to one of the most remote locations in the Galapagos in search of giant tortoises, and boards a submersible to dive for a new species(n)

programme.title: Animals at Play
programme.desc: Part one of two. The reasons why young animals enjoy playing, and how it can teach them to survive in the wild. The programme explores how young elephants, which rarely leave their mothers' side for the first eight years of life, play to develop social skills, while macaques hone their mental agility by playing games with stones. Plus, a zoologist who looks after orphaned cheetahs reveals how play can help cubs learn to hunt(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Road to Mexico
programme.desc: The chef heads across one of the busiest border crossings in the world - San Diego to Tijuana - for the start of his Mexican adventures. He follows the Pacific coastline down to Ensenada, and samples flour tortilla burritos, fresh fish, marinated and cooked on Popotla Beach, and visits the Valle de Guadalupe - Mexico's best kept secret for fabulous wines(n)

programme.title: Live Cycling
programme.desc: Clare Balding presents live coverage of the annual race, as the riders complete 3.4km circuits in central London. WNT-Rotor Pro Cycling Team's Kirsten Wild prevailed last year, with the Dutch rider managing to avoid a late crash to outsprint her rivals and become the first woman to win this race for a second time, having also been victorious in 2016. However, she is expected to face stiff competition this time around, as leading teams such as Boels-Dolmans, Trek-Segafredo and Team Sunweb are in attendance(n)

programme.title: Proms Encore
programme.desc: Katie Derham is joined by Australian tenor Stuart Skelton and Finnish violinist Pekka Kuusisto for the Proms companion show. The Rev Richard Coles receives a tutorial from Notre Dame organist Olivier Latry and there is a piano and cello performance by sibling stars Sheku and Isata Kanneh-Mason(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: Mainwaring and his platoon set up an observation post in the local lighthouse, but manage to annoy ARP warden Hodges by inadvertently flooding the whole town with light during an air raid. Comedy first shown in 1970, starring Arthur Lowe and John Le Mesurier(n)

programme.title: Animal Babies: First Year on Earth
programme.desc: Cameras follow six baby animals as they learn the basic skills needed to face the challenges of surviving their first year on Earth. In Kenya, new-born elephant calf Safina learns how to keep up with her fast moving herd, and in Sri Lanka, one-week-old Jazir has to quickly learn the ropes of a primate society. At the edge of the Arctic, five-week-old fox cubs must learn to hunt before their siblings beat them to limited food, and in Uganda, an eight-week-old mountain gorilla adapts to forest life(n)

programme.title: Princess Margaret: The Rebel Royal
programme.desc: Part one of two. Documentary profiling the Queen's sister, whose life and loves reflected the social and sexual revolution that transformed Britain during the 20th century. With archive footage and interviews, the series follows Margaret's life as she redefined the image of the modern princess, and begins with her birth in 1930. The abdication of her uncle propelled Margaret to second in line to the throne, but her life would be shaped by dramatic social change as Britain entered a more egalitarian era after the Second World War(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Part one of two. Celebrating LGBT+ stand-up comedy at the BBC, showcasing the funniest moments from stars who have appeared on Live at the Apollo. This edition features sets from Fern Brady, Desiree Burch, Alan Carr, Larry Dean, Eddie Izzard, Joe Lycett and Zoe Lyons(n)

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: Van and her friends score exclusive invitations to rapper Drake's New Year's Eve party and have big hopes of meeting the host. However, things do not turn out as planned. Comedy drama, starring Zazie Beetz(n)

programme.title: Atlanta
programme.desc: After getting into an argument with his girlfriend, Paper Boi storms off, gets mugged, gets lost in the woods, and meets a creepy old man who will not stop following and taunting him. Comedy drama, starring Brian Tyree Henry(n)

programme.title: Golf: Women's British Open
programme.desc: Eilidh Barbour presents action from the third day of the event, which this year is staged at Woburn Golf Club in Buckinghamshire. The course last played host to this event in 2016 and saw Thailand's Ariya Jutanugarn claim victory. Georgia Hall won the 2018 tournament at Royal Lytham & St Annes, becoming the first English player to win the prestigious title since Karen Stupples in 2004(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Bruno Brookes introduce the edition first aired on April 14, featuring Bananarama, Natalie Cole, Climie Fisher, Def Leppard, S'Express, T'Pau, Jermaine Stewart, Jellybean featuring Adele Bertei, Pebbles, Pet Shop Boys and Bros(n)

programme.title: Skies Above Britain
programme.desc: The complex and hidden world of the skies above us and the human stories of the people who spend their life in flight, beginning with how the skies are safeguarded. Air traffic controllers deal with an unidentified aircraft flying across Gatwick's flight path and the RAF scramble a typhoon jet to intercept an unresponsive plane. Elsewhere, two vintage plane enthusiasts explore their continued passion for flight, despite having faced personal tragedy in the air(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Rockpools
programme.desc: Palaeontologist Professor Richard Fortey explores the species that live within the seaside water formations, some of which have existed for millions of years. He is joined by some of the UK's leading marine biologists at the National Marine Aquarium in Plymouth to reveal new insights into the creatures' lives(n)

programme.title: Woolly Mammoth: Secrets from the Ice
programme.desc: Alice Roberts explores the natural history of the Ice Age mammals that once roamed across what is now Europe and Asia. Detailed information about their lives has been gleaned from carcasses preserved in the Siberian permafrost, and the anatomist meets scientists using genetic, chemical and molecular tests to reveal the adaptations that allowed mammoths to evolve from tropical animals to creatures capable of surviving extreme cold(n)

programme.title: Size Matters
programme.desc: Hannah Fry imagines a world in which things are smaller. She finds out what would happen if the Earth was half the size it is now, shrinks people to just 5mm tall and finds out the minimum size of the sun and other stars(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Steve Wright and Bruno Brookes introduce the edition first aired on April 14, featuring Bananarama, Natalie Cole, Climie Fisher, Def Leppard, S'Express, T'Pau, Jermaine Stewart, Jellybean featuring Adele Bertei, Pebbles, Pet Shop Boys and Bros(n)

programme.title: The Secret History of My Family
programme.desc: The documentary charting social history through family trees begins in 1890s Salford, where press hysteria and public panic gripped the town as rival gangs were pitched in street battles. Local magistrate Joseph Makinson was determined to stamp out this gang warfare, known as scuttling. This film tells his story and that of the gang leaders he came up against, and also learns about their descendants, revealing whether violence is still part of life in working-class Salford(n)

programme.title: The Genius of Marie Curie - The Woman Who Lit Up the World
programme.desc: Polish physicist and chemist Marie Curie became a celebrity during her lifetime, attracting media attention for being the first woman to win a Nobel Prize. This docu-drama looks at the woman behind the science, revealing a tenacious mother who had to survive the pain of the loss of husband and collaborator Pierre and the public humiliation of a doomed love affair, but who also discovered two elements and coined the term radioactivity. David Malone narrates, with readings by Geraldine James(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of Rockpools
programme.desc: Palaeontologist Professor Richard Fortey explores the species that live within the seaside water formations, some of which have existed for millions of years. He is joined by some of the UK's leading marine biologists at the National Marine Aquarium in Plymouth to reveal new insights into the creatures' lives(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: BBC Proms 2019
programme.desc: Katie Derham introduces two pieces originally premiered by Proms founder Sir Henry Wood. Here, Leif Ove Andsnes performs Britten's Piano Concerto, while Claudia Mahnke and Stuart Skelton sing Mahler's poignant Das Lied von der Erde. Edward Gardner conducts the BBC Symphony Orchestra(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Simon Bates present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on April 21 and featuring performances by S'Express, George Michael, Danny Wilson, Pat and Mick, Will Downing, James Brown, Patrick Swayze and Wendy Frazer, Jermaine Stewart, Hazell Dean, Pet Shop Boys, Michael Jackson and the Jackson Five(n)

programme.title: Ibiza: The Silent Movie
programme.desc: Director Julien Temple and soundtrack provider Fatboy Slim offer a musical time-travelling trip through the history of dance music's most influential, charismatic and spiritual island in the Mediterranean, taking in 3,000 years of its history. Ibiza's bohemian heart is now facing its strongest challenge yet, to continue to beat strongly in the face of the ever growing annual invasion of wealthy socialites and the gentrification of the island in the name of progress(n)

programme.title: Everybody in the Place: An Incomplete History of Britain 1984-1992
programme.desc: Jeremy Deller contends that the explosion of acid house and rave in the UK was a reaction to a much wider and deeper set of fault lines in British culture. Here, the Turner Prize-winning artist upturns popular notions of the musical genres as rare and unseen archive materials map the journey from protest movements to abandoned warehouse raves, the white heat of industry bleeding into the chaotic release of the dancefloor(n)

programme.title: Can You Feel It – How Dance Music Conquered the World
programme.desc: The story of the dance revolution, following the four-on-the-floor rhythm pattern beat from its disco origins, through remix culture in to house, techno and acid house. The steady, uniformly accented beat was originally used in disco and electronic dance music, but was later adopted by the house genre. The charts are packed with such tunes made or remixed by superstar DJs and the irresistible and relentless groove of the dance floor fills clubs and stadiums. Narrated by Rosalind Eleazar(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1988
programme.desc: Peter Powell and Simon Bates present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on April 21 and featuring performances by S'Express, George Michael, Danny Wilson, Pat and Mick, Will Downing, James Brown, Patrick Swayze and Wendy Frazer, Jermaine Stewart, Hazell Dean, Pet Shop Boys, Michael Jackson and the Jackson Five(n)

programme.title: Rollermania: Britain's Biggest Boy Band
programme.desc: Members of the classic Bay City Rollers line-up, including Les McKeown, recall how they went from humble beginnings playing pubs and clubs across Scotland to achieving pop stardom in the 1970s. They also reveal the dark side that dogged the band behind the scenes, including their relationship with manager Tam Paxton, bankruptcy, legal battles and division between the band members. Featuring rarely seen archive footage from the era, plus contributions from the band's songwriters, producers and record company promoters(n)

programme.title: The Last Seabird Summer?
programme.desc: Part two of two. Adam Nicolson catches up with the seabirds of the Shiants in July, and the colonies seem to be thriving. The puffings guillemots and razorbills are bringing in bill-fuls of fish to feed their chicks, but he discovers this scene is not a common one. The sea is failing to provide the birds with the food they need to survive, and in Scotland alone, an estimated 40 per cent of seabirds have been lost. Some olonies in Iceland are reported to have been wiped out, and Adam speaks to puffin hunters to discover how they have responded to this problem. Plus, with the help of expert ornithologists and marine scientists, he gains an insight into the global forces driving the crisis(n)

programme.title: New Zealand: Earth's Mythical Islands
programme.desc: Sam Neill narrates a documentary examining the wildlife of the Antipodean island chain, which has been left to its own devices for 80 million years - with surprising consequences. The opening edition features New Zealand's weirdest and most ancient species, including dinosaur-like reptiles, insects that have become giants, and flightless birds that behave like badgers, while the people who settled developed their own unique culture, and all must battle the natural forces that shape the island nation(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: Now on extended leave from the police, Philip Norgaard is contacted by June al-Baqee, a Danish national who has been prosecuted for fighting against ISIS in Syria, who says she has evidence of Danish involvement in the Syrian War. However, June is kidnapped as she waits for the detective. Return of the thriller, starring Johannes Lassen(n)

programme.title: Below the Surface
programme.desc: Philip follows June's trail to the ferry between Denmark and Sweden and warns the captain that a kidnapping is taking place onboard and that he should activate the silent alarm. In Danish(n)

programme.title: Merely Marvelous - The Dancing Genius of Gwen Verdon
programme.desc: A celebration of the art and life of the Broadway star, who overcame obstacles including rickets, the Hollywood system and a difficult marriage to choreographer and director Bob Fosse to become a multi-Tony Award winning performer. Verdon's life is told through interviews with family members and theatre associates as well rare footage from her stage and screen career(n)

programme.title: Sinatra: All or Nothing at All
programme.desc: Up-close and personal examination of the life, music and career of legendary entertainer Frank Sinatra, told in his own words from hours of archived interviews, along with commentary from those closest to him. Featuring previously unseen footage from home movies and concert performances, including his legendary 1971 'retirement concert' in Los Angeles, this tribute to the showman follows Sinatra's growth from roadhouse performer to global singing sensation(n)

programme.title: Where in the World?
programme.sub-title: Hamza,Truc and Anushka: Truc has a little sleep - at school
programme.desc: Stories about the everyday lives of ordinary children living in eight different countries around the world. In this programme we visit three children who are all having a very busy day. In Jordan, seven-year-old Hamza is having trouble getting up for school. But will he get there in time? At school in Vietnam, Truc, who's seven, plays lots of games, has a tasty lunch and a little sleep with her friends. And after school in India, six-year-old Anushka learns how to draw patterns on the ground called Rangoli with her aunt. In each film we see that although children live in different countries, may look different and speak different languages, the joys and challenges of childhood are universal(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

Away from a high energy house hunt, Margherita discovers how one Dales company has recently been instrumental in reviving Britain's hand-made piano heritage, an echo of production glory days during the 1920s(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Winchester, Hampshire, busy doctor Carolyn wants a tranquil spot to help her escape the stresses of her high-pressured job. Her 'happy place' has always been by the beach, where she has fond memories of going as a child with her grandparents, so Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers are tasked with making her dream come true. It'll be a tough challenge however, as there are very few living plants in her garden to start with(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil's family wait for news as his life continues to hang in the balance. Karen and Caren share their first kiss, while Habiba is disappointed when Adam cancels their date - and things prove awkward when he and Honey arrive at the restaurant. Rainie is hurt when Stuart takes her to a Narcotics Anonymous meeting and Denise gets more than she bargained for when Patrick returns home with an unexpected guest(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Olympic long jump gold medallist Greg Rutherford, former World Superbikes title-winner James Toseland, world champion swimmer Karen Pickering and Great Britain gymnast Amy Tinkler join regular team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: Hold the Sunset
programme.desc: The late-in-life comedy starring Alison Steadman and John Cleese returns for its second run. With their retirement together waiting on the sale of his house, Phil despairs of finding the right estate agent. The current one suggests an 'open day', but Phil baulks at the idea. Edith, meanwhile, is heartened to find that her son Roger is earning some sort of a living, even if it's only busking. Queenie confesses to Edith that she can't bear the thought of her going away, and when she hears that plans are currently stalled by the difficulty of selling Phil's house, she sees her chance to influence events(n)

programme.title: Still Game
programme.desc: It's last orders at the Clansman as Greg Hemphill and Ford Kiernan prepare to say a fond farewell to their popular characters for good in the final episode. But before they do, Jack and Victor have one last adventure. It sees the OAP duo decide to climb the world-famous munro Ben Lomond - and invite Tam, Winston and Boabby to come with them. With excuses thin on the ground, the gang reluctantly join the duo on their outdoor adventure. Will they regret the decision?(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Comedian Tudur Owen rewinds the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. This time he's on a quest to discover the truth about food. It's a story of tofu, bean baths and hedgehog crisps, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: Warriors: Our Homeless World Cup
programme.desc: The Warriors have landed in Mexico City for the Homeless World Cup. They may not know all the rules of the beautiful game or the words to their national anthem - but together, they're a force to be reckoned with. The team members are determined and full of optimism, but it takes a while to hit their stride. It's not only rivals on the pitch they have to worry about, but also sickness and injuries. The pressure is on and they're about to face some formidable opposition - Brazil, Chile, Columbia and their arch enemies - England(n)

programme.title: Killing Eve
programme.desc: Eve's mission is disastrously compromised. Meanwhile, Carolyn briefs Eve on her current status as an employee of the British government and leaves her future in her own hands. Villanelle does everything she can in order to reach her ultimate target. Spy thriller, starring Jodie Comer and Sandra Oh(n)

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The team re-examines the murder of a junior doctor strangled in 2010. Interviewing her relatives, who are all doctors, staff at her hospital and a surgeon who had been the young woman's tutor, officers hope to identify a lead that was missed during the original investigation. Depressed at the thought of his daughter Holly leaving home for university, Danny finds himself being cheered by his colleagues as they hire a pedibus for a ride through central London. Tamzin Outhwaite and Nicholas Lyndhurst star(n)

Pixar animated adventure, with the voices of Raymond Ochoa and Jack Bright(n)

programme.desc: An Irish woman living with her mother in the 1950s decides to make a fresh start in America. She makes her home in New York, and falls in love. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: With Phil left for dead, those involved take drastic action to cover their tracks - but how far are they willing to go to keep their name out of the frame? Bernadette grows frustrated as Karen and Caren continue their date and forces her mum to think about what she is doing. Stuart and Rainie go out for dinner, but he becomes increasingly concerned about her behaviour. Adam invites Habiba out, only for Honey to suggest they spend some time together(n)

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals - a tough time for some, but for others a food bonanza. Jasari and his wild dog family are under siege from the hyena clan, so he must train his pups to survive, while Bakari the baboon is living in exile from the troop, only to spy the leader bullying his adopted baby. Kali and her cubs are enjoying life back in the heart of the pride, only to be attacked by the black-maned lions. But worse is to come when a storm ignites a devastating fire, consuming the dry land with flames(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actress Naomie Harris's dramatic discoveries in this edition of the show in which celebrities research their family trees inspires her to anger, sadness and tears of joy. On a journey from Trinidad to Grenada to Jamaica, she learns of her great-great-great-great-grandfather's involvement in the exploitation of so-called liberated Africans shortly after the abolition of slavery, is delighted to discover the identity of her African five-times-great grandmother, and uncovers a family story of tragic poverty in Jamaica's capital of Kingston(n)

programme.desc: The dry season is now upon the animals - a tough time for some, but for others a food bonanza. Jasari and his wild dog family are under siege from the hyena clan, so he must train his pups to survive, while Bakari the baboon is living in exile from the troop, only to spy the leader bullying his adopted baby. Kali and her cubs are enjoying life back in the heart of the pride, only to be attacked by the black-maned lions. But worse is to come when a storm ignites a devastating fire, consuming the dry land with flames(n)

programme.title: Fake or Fortune?
programme.desc: The team examines an 18th-century portrait attributed to pioneering female artist Maria Cosway, but which Philip Mould believes may actually be an undiscovered work by the great Regency artist Sir Thomas Lawrence. He noticed it during a visit to the owners' home and now the time has come to put his hunch to the test. What's more, the difference in value is enormous - as a work by Cosway, it is worth about £8,000. If the presenter is right, it could be more in the region of half a million(n)

Edith, meanwhile, is heartened to find that her son Roger is earning some sort of a living, even if it's only busking. programme.title: BOOKtalk
programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy in discussion with Labour MP Rachel Reeves about her book Women of Westminster: The MPs Who Changed Politics. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 9 March(n)

programme.title: Royal Visit to the Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Briefings
programme.sub-title: Briefings - Theresa May on the Union

programme.title: Speaker's House

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Justice of the Supreme Court Jonathan Sumption explores how democratic processes have the power to accommodate opposition opinions and interests, but argues that in recent years politics has shied away from legislating and now the courts have taken on more and more of the role of making law(n)

programme.title: House of Commons
programme.desc: Coverage of a debate in the House of Commons on the law covering assisted dying, from Thursday 4 July(n)

programme.title: Briefings

programme.desc: Mark D'Arcy talks to Tim Bouverie about his new book 'Appeasing Hitler: Chamberlain, Churchill and the Road to War'. First shown on BBC Parliament on Saturday 13 April(n)

programme.title: Briefings
programme.sub-title: Briefings - Gordon Brown on the Union
programme.desc: Former Labour prime minister Gordon Brown making a speech in London on the future of the United Kingdom, from Tuesday 25 June(n)

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption argues that judges - especially those of the European Court of Human Rights - have usurped power by expanding the interpretation of human rights law. He explains that concepts of human rights have a long history in the common law. But by contrast, the European Convention on Human Rights has become a dynamic treaty, taking on new interpretations and powers(n)

programme.title: Select Committees
programme.desc: Coverage of the Youth Parliament committee on knife crime, focusing on intervention, with evidence from the West Midlands police, the Surrey Police and Crime Commissioner, the Scottish Violence Reduction Unit and Edward Timpson, author of the Timpson review of school exclusion, from Friday 5 July(n)

programme.title: Scottish Parliament

programme.title: Select Committees

programme.title: The Reith Lectures
programme.desc: Jonathan Sumption assesses the US and UK's constitutional models. He describes Britain's unwritten constitution as a political institution. The US Constitution is by contrast essentially a legal document. This has led Americans to address what should be political questions via the courts, rather than through politics. Recorded in front of an audience at George Washington University in Washington DC(n)

programme.title: Ross Kemp Living With
programme.sub-title: Ross Kemp Living with Young Carers
programme.desc: It is believed that one in 20 children in Britain care for sick parents or siblings. Ross Kemp meets some of these young carers, including a 15-year-old who looks after her schizophrenic mother, and an even younger boy who cares for his sister, who has cerebral palsy and visual impairment. Ross finds out how much money these young carers are saving the economy, but also considers how funding to help them is failing(n)

programme.title: Sewer Men
programme.desc: Conclusion of the two-part documentary about the sewage workers of Severn Trent. Pete and Shawn unblock the sewage well at one of the dirtiest, deepest pumping stations in Staffordshire, before dealing with a blockage outside a prison. John and Jason delve into a blockage in the sludge pump after a storm, and company boss Liv Garfield reveals how talking about her job can kick up something of a stink around the dinner table(n)

programme.desc: The comedian travels to the studios of Gaelic TV company TG4 to meet the team at an Irish-speaking weather channel. programme.title: Rescue: River Deep, Mountain High
programme.desc: On Ullswater in the Lake District, the Patterdale Mountain Rescue team is called to assist a man who has broken his leg on the far shore of the lake, the second longest in the UK. Thankfully, the team has a high-speed boat in which to rush to his aid. In the Gap of Dunloe County in Kerry, a 22-year-old climber has fallen 75ft and hit a boulder. He has regained consciousness but has broken several bones and his pelvis. The Kerry Mountain Rescue Team, National Ambulance service and Irish Coast Guard helicopter winch him 200ft on a stretcher to the hovering helicopter(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tyler's dad Jed poses a new threat to Robert. Jan attempts to throw Seb and Eileen off the scent. Jenny and Kate call a truce for Johnny's sake(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Now Murtaugh has met his match in the shape of former international CIA agent Wesley Cole, the newly melded partners attempt to find a steady rhythm as they work to solve the murder of a finance executive. Cole, who has returned home to assumes duty as an LAPD officer, also has to navigate his role with his ex and his daughter. Meanwhile, Avery grapples with the outcome of his campaign for city council. Damon Wayans, Seann William Scott and Kevin Rahm star in the fast-paced detective drama based on the hit feature films(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry(n)

The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Stan and Jack look forward to a welcome seasonal break, but on Christmas Eve a workmate phones in sick and the petulant pair are rostered to provide cover for him(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Aunt Maud buys Olive and Arthur a poodle for their 10th wedding anniversary. The dog eats Arthur's food and its fur sets him sneezing, so Olive takes it back on the bus - where the canine attacks Inspector Blake. Vintage comedy, starring Reg Varney, Anna Karen, Michael Robbins and Stephen Lewis(n)

credits.actor: Reg Varney, Anna Karen, Michael Robbins, Stephen Lewis

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The couple offer their services to a woman in labour, despite their lack of experience - though George does know how to dismantle a motorbike. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.sub-title: The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Eileen Needham sues for divorce after a domestic dispute gets out of hand, and her hard-nosed solicitor Sylvia Swinton (played by Outnumbered actress Samantha Bond) does little to calm the situation, so Joe steps in to play the peacemaker. Stan Bickle arrives in Aidensfield, bringing his daughter Josie (guest star Jenna Boyd), whose flirtatious ways steal David's heart and bring out the worst in Peggy(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Alan tells Carol off for being late again, Robert enters his first gymkhana on Treacle, Kim falls from her horse, and Frank receives a shocking revelation(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim wakes up and wants to know about the baby, Elizabeth has a go at Michael about Eric, and Mark declares he is quitting school and not taking his final exam(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The local papers report on the overturning of Ted's will, Ken tells Doug to stay away from Tracy, and Jack has enough of Vera spending money on Tom(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve phones Vicky and invites her to help him sell T-shirts at a gig, Angie hopes that Neil will move in with her, and Vera is annoyed when Social Services check up on Tom(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: Lady Virginia Revel receives a marriage proposal from an ambitious politician, but is reluctant to accept it, especially as she is also being courted by a dashing young suitor. Accompanied by Miss Marple, she travels to a weekend party at her family home Chimneys, hoping to reach a decision. Negotiations for the sale of the country house are taking place during the gathering, but when new owner Count Ludwig of Austria is found murdered, St Mary Mead's elderly spinster must identify the culprit. Whodunit, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones and Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong and Edward Fox(n)

credits.actor: Julia McKenzie, a supporting cast including Gavin & Stacey stars Ruth Jones, Mathew Horne, plus Dervla Kirwan, Charlotte Salt, Michelle Collins, Jonas Armstrong, Edward Fox

programme.title: Downton Abbey
programme.desc: Branson's political views land him in trouble and Sybil's loyalty is tested to the limit, while Ethel makes a difficult decision about her son's future. Anna wonders what is causing Bates' silence, and Carson begins recruiting a footman, with promising candidate Jimmy (Ed Speleers, Eragon) sparking immediate interest below stairs. Costume drama, starring Allen Leech, Jessica Brown Findlay, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt, Brendan Coyle and Jim Carter(n)

credits.actor: Allen Leech, Jessica Brown Findlay, Amy Nuttall, Joanne Froggatt, Brendan Coyle, Jim Carter

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica puts her investigative skills to the test once more, this time delving into two suspicious deaths at a vineyard. Starring Angela Lansbury(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Hathaway appears to have got over his fear of Lewis treading over his newly promoted toes. Their opposites have attracted in the past, and there seems no reason to suspect that they won't now the younger man has got used to the idea of his former mentor coming out of retirement to lend him a hand. Even DS Maddox isn't getting on Hathaway's nerves quite as much, so this rejuvenated trio sets out to discover who murdered American classics student Rose Anderson, whose body has been hauled from the canal with neck and abdomen wounds. Nevertheless, a few red herrings threaten to get in their way of landing the killer. Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox and Angela Griffin star(n)

credits.actor: Kevin Whately, Laurence Fox, Angela Griffin

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: First episode of the drama, starring Michael Kitchen as a jaded police superintendent who would rather be fighting the enemy in France than crime on the south coast as the Second World War gathers pace in 1940. However, when a local magistrate's German wife is murdered amid a growing cloud of uncertainty and anti-Nazi feeling, the ensuing investigation is anything but routine. Edward Fox, Robert Hardy, David Horovitch, Anthony Howell and Honeysuckle Weeks co-star(n)

credits.actor: Edward Fox, Robert Hardy, David Horovitch, Anthony Howell, Honeysuckle Weeks co-

programme.title: Carry On Behind
programme.desc: Professors Ronald Crump and Anna Vooshka embark on an archaeological dig, but find their efforts disrupted by the saucy shenanigans of a motley bunch of holiday-makers in the neighbouring caravan park. Comedy, starring Kenneth Williams, Elke Sommer, Joan Sims, Windsor Davies, Kenneth Connor and Bernard Bresslaw(n)

credits.actor: Kenneth Williams, Elke Sommer, Joan Sims, Windsor Davies, Kenneth Connor, Bernard Bresslaw

programme.title: Cilla
programme.desc: Sheridan Smith takes the lead role in Jeff Pope's three-part drama charting Cilla Black's rise to fame in the 1960s. As the series opens, Priscilla White is a Liverpool typist with dreams of escaping the office and becoming a singer. It looks like her chance has come when she meets songwriter Bobby Willis, who claims to be in the music industry - so she's less than impressed to later discover his flash car is hired and he actually works in a bakery. Despite this inauspicious start, Cilla agrees to let him manage her, but will their arrangement still stand after her friends, a local band by the name of the Beatles, help her land an audition with genuine impresario Brian Epstein? Aneurin Barnard, Ed Stoppard and John Henshaw also star(n)

credits.actor: Aneurin Barnard, Ed Stoppard, John Henshaw also

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: A year has passed since the fire that engulfed Leopard's Den destroyed the animal hospital and left Danny a widower. Now, the vet faces further upheaval as the court decides whether he can maintain custody of stepson Evan. Elsewhere, Rosie attends an interview for vet school and Du Plessis is forced to take the law into his own hands to save a lone baby elephant. Stephen Tompkinson, Lucy-Jo Hudson, Luke Ward-Wilkinson and Deon Stewardson star(n)

credits.actor: Stephen Tompkinson, Lucy-Jo Hudson, Luke Ward-Wilkinson, Deon Stewardson

programme.title: Wild at Heart
programme.desc: Danny and Evan find a sick hippo while on the run and risk being discovered in their bid to save it. Rosie meets Vanessa, the glamorous new owner of Mara who needs help with a lioness, and her only option is to act illegally to treat the animal. Georgina offers a delighted Fatani a job and De Lange agrees to fund Leopard's Den. Stephen Tompkinson, Luke Ward-Wilkinson and Lucy-Jo Hudson star(n)

credits.actor: Stephen Tompkinson, Luke Ward-Wilkinson, Lucy-Jo Hudson

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack are challenged to a darts match by canny clippies Iris and Jenny - who compensate for a lack of skill by using their feminine charms to gain an unfair advantage(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Mum and Olive are suffering from the flu _ leaving Stan and Arthur to do all the chores(n)

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service(n)

programme.title: The Royal
programme.desc: Nigel Harper prepares for the arrival of an important patient, who proves useful when an injured teenager is brought in. Lizzie comes to terms with her husband's recent behaviour. Medical drama following the staff and patients at a hospital in Sixties Yorkshire, starring John Axon, Linda Armstrong and Michelle Hardwick(n)

credits.actor: John Axon, Linda Armstrong, Michelle Hardwick

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Cash-strapped George knows exactly what he can sell to raise money - but decides not to tell Mildred. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: The Ropers' bed collapses and George finds himself short of cash to acquire a new one. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: An old flame of Mildred's turns up at the Roper house, prompting George to blow the cobwebs off his little black book. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: A woman is found murdered near a driving school and the investigation proves a testing time for Morse and especially Lewis, who has the added distraction of being paired with an attractive female colleague. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Kevin Whately, with Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak and Christopher Fulford(n)

credits.actor: John Thaw, Kevin Whately, Patrick Malahide, Tessa Wojtczak, Christopher Fulford

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall(n)

credits.actor: Julia McKenzie, a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer, Rik Mayall

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A burglar dubbed 'The Creeper' targets the villages of Midsomer as Barnaby and Jones investigate the murder of a guest at an upper-crust dinner party. As the case progresses, Barnaby becomes the thief's latest victim and it emerges that several high-society friends with connections to gangsters and a police chief have a few secrets worth killing for. Detective drama, starring John Nettles and Jason Hughes, with guest appearances from Rik Mayall and Jenny Agutter(n)

credits.actor: John Nettles, Jason Hughes, Rik Mayall, Jenny Agutter

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: A mysterious fire that killed an artist with links to political activists leaves the police baffled, while Bright finds himself short-staffed when a stomach bug puts most of his men on sick leave. The situation becomes more serious when a housewife dies of the same condition. Endeavour investigates a family-run supermarket, believing the infection may have stemmed from their stock being deliberately contaminated, and discovers dark secrets that may have a connection to the unexplained blaze(n)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: An armed robbery ends in murder and Frost wastes no time in arresting a known crook he saw at the scene during the getaway, convinced he is one of the men responsible. With this little mystery to solve, as well as a case of four burglaries in as many days at the same house, the dogged policeman's lot proves a busy one indeed. David Jason stars, with Billy Murray and James Hayes(n)

programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jack is exasperated as Vera determines to pay for Lisa's funeral, Vera is stunned when Terry tells her he does not want Tommy, and Tracy arranges to meet Doug(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: The Council drop the one-way system, Sally is congratulated for her campaign but she feels it is not much consolation for Lisa, and Tracy tries to seduce Doug(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Joe and George call a meeting of local farmers to bring to an end their harassment of a neighbour whose unacceptable farming practices have made him deeply unpopular. Dawn falls for good-looking newcomer Billy Garrett, but discovers his charming manner conceals a tortured past that produces sudden angry outbursts. David is hired to help Peggy compile an inventory of house contents of a recently deceased woman, but fears his companion's dodgy dealings have upset the restless spirit of the former resident. Guest starring Phyllis Logan and Tim Brooke-Taylor(n)

programme.title: Murder, She Wrote
programme.desc: Jessica and her friends are amused by the drama unfolding before their eyes on TV - until they realise they are watching a real killing being carried out. Crime drama, starring Angela Lansbury(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Olive becomes convinced Arthur is having an affair with a conductress at the depot. Comedy, with Reg Varney, Anna Karen and Michael Robbins(n)

programme.title: On the Buses
programme.desc: Stan and Jack decide to get fit, disturbed to learn that bus crews have a short life expectancy. Comedy, with Reg Varney(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: Poor Mildred's hopes are raised when work-shy George looks for a job, but his chances of finding gainful employment seem slim. Comedy, starring Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Armed robbers target post offices around Aidensfield and Mason finds himself working with old flame Rachel Dawson, whose career is under threat following an affair with a senior colleague. programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack hatches a plan to help Michael get his ban from the Woolpack lifted, and Eric decides to do something about improving his relationship with Michael(n)

programme.desc: Archie offers to take Peter for a picnic, but Lynn refuses, Melanie insists that Mark sits his English exam, and Seth offers to be a spy for the Woolpack tug o' war team(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Gerald Hadleigh, the secretary of the local writers' circle in the tranquil village of Midsomer Worthy, is found battered to death on the eve of a visit by a best-selling novelist. programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: The evacuation of a central London hospital leads to the discovery of a tragic death. Patrick Malahide and Bill Paterson co-star(n)

programme.title: Law & Order: UK
programme.desc: Brooks and Devlin fail to find a lead when a 13-year-old is kicked to death at Euston Station, so they turn to Chandler, who holds a press conference. As the detectives are led through a tangled web of family loyalties, the suspect's barrister, an old flame of Steel's, gathers evidence for an audacious defence strategy that could change the nature of the British legal system. Dervla Kirwan guest stars, with Bradley Walsh, Jamie Bamber and Ben Daniels(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: As the sole witness to a dying man's last words, Bobby Attfield is determined to solve the riddle they present, and forms an unlikely alliance with socialite Frankie Derwent and visiting family friend Miss Marple to help him on his quest. A trail of clues leads them to Castle Savage, where they uncover a hotbed of stifled emotion, treachery and deceit among its strange inhabitants. Murder mystery, starring Julia McKenzie, with a supporting cast that includes Sean Biggerstaff, Samantha Bond, Richard Briers, Rafe Spall, Warren Clarke, Mark Williams, Georgia Moffett, Helen Lederer and Rik Mayall(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.desc: The glamorous Ruth Van Rydock contacts her old friend Jane Marple with a mystery - her sister Carrie Louise's study has been devastated by a suspicious fire. While Carrie Louise has the same cheerful outlook as ever, Ruth fears dark forces are at play at Stonygates, the home Carrie shares with her third husband, various relatives and a motley crew of young criminals, the latest subject of Carrie's philanthropic endeavours. But on the evening before a theatrical show is staged for their guests, one of whom is Miss Marple, an even more fascinating performance takes place before their very eyes - one where the final act is murder. Julia McKenzie stars, with guest appearances from Joan Collins, Penelope Wilton, Brian Cox, Maxine Peake, Alexei Sayle, Sarah Smart, Ian Ogilvy, Sean Hughes and Nigel Terry(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Marple
programme.sub-title: The Mirror Crack'd from Side to Side
programme.desc: Hollywood actress Marina Gregg and her director husband take up residence at Gossington Hall in the village of St Mary Mead and hold a reception to meet their new neighbours, including the hall's previous owner Dolly Bantry. The celebrations turn to tragedy, however, after a fan fatally drinks a poisoned cocktail apparently meant for the new lady of the manor. But a different picture begins to emerge as Marina spits out a similarly spiked cup of coffee on the set of her new film - and Miss Marple investigates the star's unhappy past. Julia McKenzie stars, with Lindsay Duncan, Nigel Harman, Joanna Lumley, Martin Jarvis, Hugh Bonneville and Michele Dotrice(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: First episode of the drama, starring Michael Kitchen as a jaded police superintendent who would rather be fighting the enemy in France than crime on the south coast as the Second World War gathers pace in 1940. However, when a local magistrate's German wife is murdered amid a growing cloud of uncertainty and anti-Nazi feeling, the ensuing investigation is anything but routine. programme.title: Inside
programme.desc: The inner workings of the famous retailer, which set the standard for quality clothing and introduced the UK to everything from chicken Kievs to Percy Pig. It also considers how, as the retail market has changed, the brand has struggled in recent years(n)

programme.title: Portillo: The Trouble with the Tories
programme.desc: Part one of two. Former Defence Secretary Michael Portillo examines divisions within the Conservative Party. He looks at events leading up to David Cameron's decision to promise an in/out EU referendum, and the subsequent Brexit crisis that has followed. He also explores how the referendum campaign in 2016 led to the downfall of Cameron's government and a battle for control of the Tories(n)

programme.title: Freddie Mercury: The Greatest Showman

However, Shaun Williamson opts instead to visit the grave of James Brindley, the man who designed the tunnel, but never saw it completed. After joining the Macclesfield Canal, Amanda and Anita head to a barge-based artist's studio for a gentle afternoon of sketching, while Shaun and Michael head to 18th-century folly Mow Cop. programme.title: When... Goes Horribly Wrong

programme.desc: When holidays go right they offer much-needed rest and relaxation in the lap of luxury. However, those same vacations can just as easily end in disaster. Examples include chartered accountant Bill and his wife Tanya, who paid £11,000 on a once-in-a-lifetime cruise to the Bahamas, only to end up trapped in the eye of a cyclone. Iain and Judie's luxury dream trip to Vegas turned into deadly nightmare when they were caught up in the now infamous shootings in October 2017 in which 58 people were killed.
